_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of implementing enterprise key escrow systems to support data-at-rest encryption.
reference_document_element_description: Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topology, data domain structure, integration patterns, and data lifecycle policies.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-013 (LS, cosine: 0.429), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.420).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.1/10 (raw 0.208). Shared vocabulary: [data, enterprise]. DCWF-only terms [encryption, escrow, implementing, key, knowledge, rest] vs WIDAI-only terms [architecture, design, document, domain, integration, lifecycle] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.873 (P=0.877, R=0.869). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Knowledge of implementing enterprise key escrow systems to support data-at-rest" and "Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topolog" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.434 > STS 0.208 (gap +0.226). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, enterprise] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.4%. Shared tokens: [data, enterprise]. DCWF-only: [encryption, escrow, implementing, key, knowledge, rest]. WIDAI-only: [architecture, design, document, domain, integration, lifecycle].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.226 with low STS 0.208. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
